title: [Bug]: Security label array items can contain the semicolon separator
summary: Security label list values supplied as JSON arrays can contain semicolons, creating ambiguous scalar rendering and downstream interpretation.
observed: A security label list item such as 'REL-A;REL-B' is accepted as a single list item.
expected: Array-supplied security label list items should reject the semicolon separator. Semicolon-separated strings should continue to be split into separate values.
reproduction: Call normalize_security_label_profile with {'releasability': ['REL-A;REL-B']} and observe validation succeeds.
failing_test: Add a unit test that expects semicolons inside security label array items to raise ValidationError.
impact: Ambiguous security label values can complicate audit review and policy checks.
delivery_semantics: ACK ordering is unchanged. Invalid metadata follows existing validation failure handling.
security: The fix improves data-centric label clarity and fail-closed parsing.
acceptance: Security label array items reject the semicolon separator.
The regression test covers releasability or handling caveat ambiguity.
No sink schema changes are introduced.
tests: Run focused security label tests and full validation.
documentation: Update release notes and test evidence.
closeout: Mark completed after merge with sanitized evidence.
